title: Parasenecio kamtschaticus

It is native to the Russian Far East and is closely related to other species within the genus Parasenecio. The genus Parasenecio is a group of plants within the tribe Senecioneae. The classification of Parasenecio kamtschaticus is further detailed within the subfamily Cichorioideae. intro: Parasenecio kamtschaticus, also known as Kamchatka ragwort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Kamchatka ragwort include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, wounds, and skin irritations.
